threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still flinch when the bus splashes you as it whizzes by. Proper Hackney locals know to stand back, mate.
Heard a guy on the 133 last night promise to his mate he'd never ride a night bus again after that rat ran over his shoe. Classic Brixton.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: I knew I was home when I saw a girl in a dirndl ordering falafel at 2 AM while the kebab shop blasted techno. Classic Neukölln moment.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a currywurst stand with a rotating DJ set. It's the perfect mix of street food and Berlin nightlife, truly unique.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You know someone’s from the Mission if they can name five bars on Valencia and still complain about the taco truck lines being too long.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught the 19 this morning, and the driver yelled 'next stop, the Land of Misfit Toys!' as someone hopped on with a parrot on their shoulder.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think ‘tapas’ means small plates everywhere; locals know you ask for ‘pintxos’ at the bar next door for the good stuff.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You can tell by their bewilderment at the tram schedules—often staring at the signs like they’re hieroglyphs. Classic sign of a newcomer.

threadTitle: Things that instantly reveal you just moved here
body: You still think the 106 bus is the way to get around instead of mastering the train lines. It's a rookie mistake, mate.
Caught a Night Bus 133 the other night. A guy debated loudly with his empty can of cider about existentialism. Brixton’s late-night charm is unmatched.

threadTitle: When you knew you were actually living here
body: When I saw the guy in a full suit, arguing passionately with a pigeon over a piece of bread near the Maybachufer. That's Neukölln for you.

threadTitle: Things Berlin does that nowhere else does
body: Only in Kreuzberg can you find a late-night currywurst stand that also doubles as a gallery. Art hangs on the walls, but the ketchup is still questionable.

threadTitle: Real signs someone is from here vs just working here
body: You can spot a local by their eye roll when tourists block the sidewalk taking selfies in front of the same street art for the hundredth time.

threadTitle: Muni moments that define this city
body: Caught a whiff of fresh pastries from a nearby bakery while stuck at Civic Center. Dog on the tracks? More like a cafe break for the drivers.

threadTitle: Things tourists get wrong about living here
body: Tourists think all of Gràcia is about fancy bars—most locals still grab their morning café at the same corner spot for decades, far from the crowds.

threadTitle: How to spot a recent arrival
body: You'll see them struggling with the metro map, often staring at it like it's a treasure map, while locals navigate effortlessly.
